message:
perf(raptorq): add fused dual-slice GF(256) SIMD mul/addmul for AVX2 and NEON

Replace sequential pairs of single-slice SIMD operations with fused
dual-slice variants that process both slices in a single pass:

gf256_mul_slices2: New gf256_mul_slices2_x86_avx2_impl_tables and
gf256_mul_slices2_aarch64_neon_impl_tables fused implementations.
Each iteration loads and processes both dst_a and dst_b within the
same loop body, sharing the lookup table broadcasts and nibble mask
setup. Tails (uneven lengths) fall back to single-slice impl.

gf256_addmul_slices2: New gf256_addmul_slices2_x86_avx2_impl_tables
and gf256_addmul_slices2_aarch64_neon_impl_tables. Same fused pattern
for additive multiply: both (dst_a,src_a) and (dst_b,src_b) pairs
processed per iteration.

This reduces loop overhead, improves instruction-level parallelism,
and keeps both slice pairs hot in L1 cache simultaneously.

Adds equivalence tests verifying fused dual-lane implementations
produce identical results to sequential single-lane calls, including
uneven-length remainder handling (97/161 and 95/157 byte pairs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
